meta.name: Workstream-tagged 5-role replay (chat-declutter polish)
meta.description: Deterministic 5-role replay with three workstreams pre-declared (containment / comms / legal). Exercises the iter-4 chat-declutter UI: colored track-bar stripes, per-message workstream tags, structural @-mentions on AI broadcasts (including CISO addressing), critical-inject lifecycle, and mixed active-set narrowing across three play turns. Useful for QA-ing the polish PR without driving a real LLM. No live model calls — every AI message is baked in.

scenario_prompt: Ransomware via compromised vendor portal, mid-size bank.

tool_args.message: EXERCISE START. Beat 1 — Detection & Triage. Jordan and Bo own this beat.

ai_messages.body:
**EXERCISE START — 03:14 UTC, Wednesday**

Your EDR fired ransomware-encryption signatures across a cluster of finance-team laptops. At least four file shares are now serving back `.lockbit`-suffixed files. Initial intel links to a shared service account tied to a third-party billing-portal vendor breached two weeks ago — the password was never rotated.

Month-end finance close is in progress. No ground-truth on data exfiltration yet.

**Beat 1 — Detection & Triage**

Two questions the team needs answered right now:

1. **Which hosts are actively encrypting?** Scope, patient zero, lateral indicators.
2. **Is the service account still live?** Has it authenticated anywhere else in the last 24 hours?

**Jordan (IR Lead)** — take point on triage. What's your first action?

**Bo (SOC)** — pull EDR alert queue + service account auth logs.

submissions.content: On it. Spinning up the war room. Defender on EDR, Okta on identity, Splunk for logs. SOC, give me a 5-minute scope read.
Initial signal: EA token used to enumerate the M365 directory at 02:14, then a Cobalt-Strike-style beacon from finance-laptop-7 at 02:48. Pulling Defender + Okta logs now.

tool_args.message: Beat 2 — Scope Assessment & Containment Decision. Two threads opening.

ai_messages.body:
**Beat 2 — Scope Assessment & Containment Decision**

Good read on triage. Two threads now run in parallel:

tool_args.message: Jordan, lay out the containment sequence. Which hosts get isolated and in what order? Defender quarantine on each, confirm before moving on.

ai_messages.body: **Jordan (IR Lead)** — lay out the containment sequence. Which hosts get isolated and in what order? Defender quarantine on each, confirm before moving on.

tool_args.message: Casey, the regulator clock — when does it start, and what's our 24-hour notification template look like?

ai_messages.body: **Casey (Legal)** — the regulator clock: when does it start, and what's the 24-hour notification template look like? We may need to file before SOC's full scope read.

**EDR / Okta Telemetry — 03:14 UTC**

## Affected Hosts (confirmed encryption activity)

| Host             | User Account     | First Alert (UTC) | Status      |
|------------------|------------------|-------------------|-------------|
| finance-laptop-7 | bdunn (EA)       | 02:48             | encrypting  |
| finance-laptop-4 | smith (finance)  | 02:51             | encrypting  |
| finance-laptop-9 | jchen (finance)  | 02:53             | encrypting  |
| fileserver-fin-1 | svc_billingportal| 02:55             | mounting    |

## Service Account Activity (last 24h)

- `svc_billingportal` — 47 logins from finance VLAN; 3 logins from external IP `203.0.113.42`
- `bdunn@bank.local` — token re-used 14:22 yesterday from external; flagged but not blocked

## C2 Indicators

- Beacon: `update-cdn.azurewebsites[.]net` (typosquat — not legitimate Microsoft)
- File extension: `.lockbit` appended to Office docs, CSVs on finance shares
- No confirmed exfil yet — DLP alerts: 0 — but enumeration window (02:14–02:48) is unaccounted for

submissions.content: Containment plan: revoke the EA's tokens via Okta, rotate svc_billingportal credentials, and isolate finance-laptop-7 plus any host that authed with the EA token in the last 12 hours. SOC, confirm Defender quarantine on each as you go.
Confirm the regulator clock starts at first reasonable belief — that's now. I'll draft the 24-hr notification template. Need scope confirmation from SOC before we file.

tool_args.headline: Data exfiltration confirmed — 47 MB HR file share pulled at 03:02 UTC
tool_args.body: SOC forensics confirms the C2 beacon successfully exfiltrated approximately 47 MB from the HR file share to an external S3 bucket before network cutoff. This data likely contains employee PII. The regulator clock and disclosure posture must be reassessed immediately.

ai_messages.body: Data exfiltration confirmed — 47 MB HR file share pulled at 03:02 UTC. SOC forensics confirms the C2 beacon successfully exfiltrated approximately 47 MB from the HR file share to an external S3 bucket before network cutoff. This data likely contains employee PII. The regulator clock and disclosure posture must be reassessed immediately.

tool_args.message: Beat 3 — Stakeholder Briefing & Comms Posture. The exfil changes the calculus.

ai_messages.body:
**Beat 3 — Stakeholder Briefing & Comms Posture**

The HR file share exfil changes the calculus. We now have a confirmed PII disclosure event, not just a containment problem. Three calls need to land in the next 15 minutes of simulated time.

tool_args.message: Alex, you own the disclosure posture. Three calls needed.

ai_messages.body:
**Alex (CISO)** — you own the disclosure posture. Three calls needed:

1. Notify the regulator now, or wait for Legal's full filing?
2. Brief the board chair before or after external comms?
3. Engage law-enforcement on the exfil?

You're the decision authority. We need a written call before Comms goes external.

**Dana (Comms)** — prep two holding statements:

1. Going public at hour 4 — regulator-only notification path.
2. Going public at hour 20 — full disclosure including the HR exfil.

Don't push either without sign-off from Alex + Casey.

tool_args.message: Casey, the exfil escalates this to a notification event in every regulator we touch.

ai_messages.body: **Casey (Legal)** — the exfil escalates this to a notification event in every regulator we touch (state DOB + GLBA + SHIELD + CCPA). Update the prelim filing with the 47 MB scope and PII type confirmation. We'll need it inside 30 minutes of simulated time.

submissions.content: Decision: notify regulator at hour 4 with preliminary scope, full filing at hour 20. Brief board chair now. Comms — internal-only memo first, no external statement until Legal signs off. Engage law enforcement via outside counsel.
Internal memo released to all-staff at hour 3. Holding statement ready for press; will not push without authorization.
Concur on the timeline. Drafting prelim filing with the scope SOC confirmed (6 laptops contained, 3 pending). Will hold until board chair brief is complete.

tool_args.message: Decisions logged. Beat 4 closed; exercise wrap-up at the operator's command.

ai_messages.body:
**Decisions logged.**

- **Disclosure timing**: regulator at hour 4, full filing at hour 20.
- **Comms**: internal memo released at hour 3; holding statement queued for press, gated on Legal sign-off.
- **Law enforcement**: engaged via outside counsel.
- **Containment**: 6 of 9 laptops contained; 3 pending (executive travel — remote wipe in motion).

This closes Beat 3. The team has a written disclosure posture, a Legal filing in flight, and a containment trajectory. The exercise is at a natural wrap point — end the session when ready.
